case_001.name: Dual lane change, LC not detected (FN)
case_001.notes: Ego starts in side parking lot, pulls out and executes dual lane change with stop-and-wait (12s gap at s~78m behind parked vehicle). Deviation ramps 0->6.6m over first 60m, then plateaus at 6.1-6.6m from s=55-97m (stable in new lane). Phase 0 blocks detection: l=0.628 at s=2.89m exceeds in_lane_threshold=0.299m. If Phase 0 passed, Phase 2 would find the plateau (stddev<<0.5m, span=42m>>15m).

case_002.name: Dual lane change, different frame — ego starts near center (FN)
case_002.notes: Same clip as case_001 but different frame where ego lane is wider (2.64m). Ego starts near lane center (first points have small l). Phase 0 should pass. Deviation ramps 0->6.5m with plateau at s=55-97m (stable in new lane after first LC).

case_003.name: Second LC (left to left-left lane), wide lane — C++ TP
case_003.notes: Same clip, later frame. Ego is now in the left lane (wide, 4.1m) and executing second LC to the left-left lane. Ego starts at l=-0.94 (right of center in wide lane), crosses zero around s=14m, ramps to ~3.9m. C++ correctly detects: stable zone mean=3.26m, stddev=0.13m, distance=54m, onset=22.86m.

case_004.name: Rightward LC, narrow lane — magnitude threshold too high (FN)
case_004.notes: Ego starts near center (l=0.14), ramps rightward to l=-2.78. Narrow lane (3.43m) makes magnitude_threshold=2.716m, so deviation barely exceeds it (max |l|=2.78). Only ~15 qualifying points spanning ~14m, just under min_stable_distance=15m. Phase 2 fails.

case_005.name: Rightward LC, same clip later frame — C++ TP
case_005.notes: Same clip as case_004 but later frame. Ego further into LC. C++ detects: stable zone mean=-2.775, stddev=0.015, distance=21.9m, onset=11.93m.

case_006.name: Left LC then left turn, short trajectory due to curve cutoff (FN)
case_006.notes: Ego executes left LC then left turn. Narrow lane (2.8m), curvy road ahead (curvature=0.136) cuts trajectory to 31m. Deviation ramps 0->3.44 with peak at s=23, then decreases. Only ~33 points available. Qualifying points span ~14.6m, just under min_stable_distance=15m.

case_007.name: Out-of-lane nudge, ego departs and returns — NOT a lane change (FP)
case_007.notes: Ego starts at l=0.7, ramps left to peak l=2.73 at s=32, then returns to l~1.0 for 80+ meters. This is an out-of-lane nudge, not a permanent lane change. Phase 3 return check should catch the return using halfLaneWidth (1.63m) — ego settles at l~1.0 which is well within lane boundary.

case_008.name: Out-of-lane nudge with S-curve — left departure then rightward drift (FP)
case_008.notes: Ego starts near center, drifts slightly right (l=-0.08), then departs left (peaks at l=1.74 at s=42), returns through center at s=109, then departs far right (l=-2.37 at s=150). C++ detects rightward LC from stable zone at s=132-150 (mean=-2.32). This is actually an out-of-lane nudge — the ego lane is narrow (2.72m) and the DGPS extends 150m without lane limit cutoff. With 100m cutoff, only 7 leftward qualifying points remain (< window_size=10), so detection fails correctly.

case_009.name: Out-of-lane nudge, steady leftward departure — NOT a lane change (FP)

case_011.name: Out-of-lane nudge with double hump — ego returns within lane mid-trajectory (FP)

case_012.name: Out-of-lane nudge in wide lane, double hump with mid-return below halfLaneWidth (FP)

case_013.name: FP on curvy road with no right lane — perception drift, not lane change
case_013.notes: Updated Feb 2026: live data shows larger drift (max |l|=2.63 vs old 2.30). Old data had oscillation near threshold causing window gap; new data has monotonic drift through threshold.
